column36: Comments

column2: The Duel with the Dragon Maklu
column36: My first try totally bagelshuffled, i lost 4 duels by turn10. The second game, well, when tech works it works. During my final turn i played 18 cards, recruited 7, rescued 21 bystanders abd generated 57 attack.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: One of more advanced hero comboes, to tally attack totals after all the micro size changing, artifacts, versatiles and cheering crowds had to do heavy bookkeeping, and you constant have to keep track on twist number, to check what artifacts keep in play, and what to discard

This was a very cool set up. Initially, scheme sounded pretty insane but doc strange and voodoo had artifacts in the hero colors which helped win the duels. I could visualize these two magicians just staying out on the front lines with a force field of magical protection while the rest of the crew fought the battle. :) first game we tried to use angel noir thinking he would help with combos but he didn’t bring much power to the game which we really needed to fight the chunky villains and Foom. Second game we used Nova instead which really helped bring in some extra power and helped secure a win sooner than our first game. Still, it took awhile to get our hands refined enough to get Foom in both games. Great thematic set up! Props to Bagel!

I knew I needed to fill both decks with both Colors, so I went with Clea as she has commons in Science and Magic Colors but doesn’t have a lot of Color-based triggers. Game 1 tried Daredevil Noir but his pseudo-wall-crawl didn’t really help my speed as the game was a frustrating 39 turns long. Switched to Ms Marvel for game 2, and managed a faster win with just as much VP thanks to her Bystander engine. P2’s deck was really thin with her and rescuing loads but struggled to build enough Attack/Colors to hit Foom. P1 had a great start and some early hits with Gwen and Ms Marvel’s rares but stalled when they ran out of Bystanders for Gwen’s Cheering Crowds. It didn’t help that a Strike with 3 MU Villains in the city buried three 7-cost cards and a Staff of Legba!
Too much multi-cultural melking

It took a while to get to all hero classes and hit the mastermind. Had early legendary heroes, but otherwise bad luck drawing heroes. It was fun, because we had luck.
Second game was worse in every aspect.

It was hard to get any combos, because we needed to play around every hero class, which took away some fun.

Making the Hero Choices was a bit difficult, trying to balance the colours for the Scheme led me to Doctor Strange and Clea. Doctor Strange and Doctor Voodoo both have the advantage that some of their cards are artifacts, which mean they could help even better to avoid losing Duels. There weren't a lot of really strong Heroes available to choose from anyway, at least I didn't see any super obvious synergies.

In both my games the first 3 Duels were lost because they came before I could build my decks. In the first attempt I ended up with 4 duels being won by Fin Fang Foom, in attempt 2 that stayed at 3. Deckbuilding wise I went with a mix of everything for both decks, with the end result in attempt 1 being a deck 2 that could carry its weight with a lot of Doctor Voodoo's cards and some of Doctor Strange's card draw Common as MVPs. Deck 1 was far from useless and got great use out of one copy of Clea's Uncommon, a couple of Gwenpool's bystander Commons and one or two of Iron Man Noir's attack Commons. 

Getting to the 12 or 14 attack usually required to attack Fin Fang Foom took a lot of turns and with an average of 2-3 attack per card played, it wasn't an easy thing to do without extra draw (which was not abundant). Doctor Voodoo's Ritual Artifacts were the welcome ones to make 4+ attack reliably. Attempt 1 took 34 turns, but never felt impossible. Attempt 2 took 33, but suffered from a lot worse flow, that made awkward turns where the City was completely empty while hitting Fin Fang Foom was not doable yet. Attempt 1 also had more luck with the Bystander rescue effect from Gwenpool. She only rescued 1 bystander in attempt 2, but in attempt 1 she got 3.

The required diversified portfolio to attack Fin Fang Foom was something to struggle to get together in both games. In both games Deck 1 ended up KOing the most starters, but in attempt 2 the rest of the cards could not hit hard enough even with (almost) no starters left.. Attempt 2 saw Deck 2 recruit 4 rares, the only one escaping recruitment by a Demolish being the Iron Man Noir one, which funnily enough was the only rare recruited in attempt 1. The challenge to not lose from the Scheme was a fun one, although I became a bit anxious as the first 3 Twists were not avoidable to fail for me in both games. Luckily Twist 4-6 were by then more or less safely preventable from becoming Duels Won. In attempt 1 the last few turns were very tense as one more unlucky draw of a Scheme Twist would end in a loss, while the rest of the game felt really manageable and only the Final Blow was required. After winning, the last 2 Scheme Twists turned out to be really near the bottom, but while playing I of course couldn't know that.

Attempt 1 ended up giving me quite a few more VP than attempt 1 while being only 1 turn slower han attempt 2, so I ended up submitting that one. Doctor Strange is one of my favorite Heroes in this game. Not only do I like the character a lot, but also his Ritual Artifact cards can both be saved for a turn when you need them (whilst requiring some setup to play them again) as well as giving you some draw from his other Common. He might not get into crazy numbers or ramp like some other Heroes, but I find a card that gives you 3 attack for 3 cost  AND can be saved for a later turn has no small impact either. Clea was also fun, her Demonic Bargain comboing nicely with Iron Man Noir's 3-cost, which is on its own pretty weak, even with 3 attack, because of his downside. That downside was semi-useful in this setup too, to save cards that you needed for a Duel for a next turn to draw them guaranteed.

All in all not an easy setup, as Fin Fang Foom is no easy Mastermind, but I'm very glad it wasn't Epic Fin Fang Foom, as then I think the available heroes were simply too weak to manage that. Curious to see what week 4 will bring!
Both games went super slow. Chose Dr. Strange to have artifacts that would protect me from the scheme, but..... ugh.
First game was painful. Second game took us too the last turn to finish the Mastermind

What a slog.  Tried to lean into with artifacts to deal with the scheme but still Bagelshuffled™ my way to 4 failed duels by turn 12.  Clea's Demonic Descendant was pretty safe with her and Iron Man Noir's topdeck cards.  Big assist from Gwenpool "rescuing" Detective Wolverine and bottoming a Twist.  Would've ended 4 turns earlier but a nasty Strike slowed me down with a 4x demolish.

Pizza got the party Thor to ko cards when he bought things 5 or more turn 1 and then thinned deck really fast so what if was really easy To do. 3 masterminds at one point but our second game went by really fast
Fun setup! We find the scheme difficult to beat, but with an easier mastermind like Mr. Sinister it balances somehow. MVP was Party Thor Only Son.

column2: Cosmic Pylons and the Holy Hail
column36: Was worried with some early twists and strikes. Only a few of the twists landed on turns without focus.
Just a massive amount of card draws with sending cards undercover was really good. Twist could be annoying when you had both purchasing and attack to spend but we made it work.
Completed the game in 27 turns both play throughs. I chose heroes to boost shield level/undercover.

Was really afraid of this. Picked Thing and Sue for FF synergy, and also for a way to bypass the scheme (use focus instead of recruiting). I also thought some KO beside the undercover and testing villains would come handy. But ended up not seeing Dethlok or Sue's KO card in the first game. 
 I guess I got sort of lucky in first run. E.g. besides some strong villains I got bystanders on turns 5 and 6, and scheme twists on turn 9 and 10. So I could avoid letting villains escape and get some good cards. (Started with things 3 recruit one right away, and then could buy Mr fantastic rare for instance.) I did have 4 villains with 4 city spaces, and 3 villains with 3, so it was still not evident. My right hand could stack up some shards, and also get the Mr fantastic draw draw attack per hand size combo going quite early. So from turn 16 basically in every turn I could attack Galactus. (Artifacts and a lot of card buys really did help). Ended up with 2 city spaces remaining. (And being ready to hit G for a 6th time). 

Now, on my second play I conceded on turn 12, after the second escape, and no good synergies showing up in the HQ.

column36: One hand had a tech deck, the other a strength deck allowing for pretty easy cosmic threat triggers.

AntDude and PowerPupa had a fun 1st attempt here with a lot of loyalty to SHIELD and only some little nibbles to the planet. AntDude had a lot of Deathlok cards supported by Mr. Fantastic (but unfortunately no Gigantic Hands appeared). PowerPupa had great things going with Groot and Thing with a thin deck, lots of strength, and had Clobbering Time make at least 11 attack the three times it was played. Each were a couple attack shy of hitting Galactus twice in one turn once Cosmic Threat was off the table, but still managed a relatively quick game with no escapes. Lastly, we thoroughly quoted Galactus from Unbeatable Squirrel Girl, like "Anyway, I better go eat your planet before it gets too stale" and "A dude's gotta eat, peace!" and "Call me if you ever want to get into heralding." 5 out of 5 for the opportunity alone.

Setup was a bit too luck-based for me. Galactus, the Scheme, and Maximum Carnage all can swing really heavily one way or the other just based on luck. I lost my first game really badly, and then won my second game pretty easily.
Scarlet Spider+Mockingbird wouldn't normally be a crazy good combo, but it worked really well against Galactus. Player 1 hit galactus twice two turns in a row because of the ability to get 20+ attack and have more than 7 instinct cards.
The combo of deathlok+Mr. Fantastic was interesting. Again, not normally anything to write home about. But works well against Cosmic Threat.

Chose Drax and Mockingbird so I could have one player focus on instinct, and the other on tech. Both ended up with a good amount of tech, but player 2 also got a good amount of instinct to thin the deck. Also got Starlord's rare which gained so many shards, and secured Infinity Formula early, so the undercover ramp grew and grew. Mockingbird was also meant to help prevent unwanted feasts.
The lack of Deathlok in the HQ really dampened Player 1's potential. Reanimate took a very long time to show up. So Player one took a very long time to get anything under cover. 
The combination of mid high attacks from Maximum Carnage and High attacks from the Heralds was super tricky. I had four escapes in Game 1 due to not getting enough attack for villains, not enough henchmen, and early strikes shrinking the city. Game 2 was much friendlier with strikes, and more attack in the HQ. I never felt threatened from the scheme, which is nice cause Galactus is his own scheme. That boy hungry.
Welp. Galactus destroyed the city in 12 turns our second round so we're submitting the first one 😂

column36:
I picked White Wolf because I thought the Ambush ability to wound the Mastermind might be useful, and because Red/Black complemented Apocalyptic Black Widow. Sadly neither of those ended up mattering much. I also picked Queen Storm again due to the possibility of wounding the Mastermind, but it never came up.

I really liked this Scheme, and the way it let you engage in far more planning of your turns. I also found the combination of the Symbiote Bonding villains with Malice and Preyy, letting you farm those two Villains almost endlessly for card draw and Bystanders. The latter in particular I milked for all it was worth since one hand got Apocalyptic Black Widow's rare, which gave her "Liberate" equal to the number of Bystanders - that let her fire off 14 damage once and then 20, which was great against Thanos.

Unfortunately, in the end, this game was just kind of a slog. Super high strength Masterminds I tend to find kind of boring or frustrating because there's just too many turns where you're a few Attack short of being able to do anything. Even with careful avoidance of Master Strikes, Poison Thanos was up to 17 strength by the end, and neither deck was gelling anywhere near enough to hit him outside of ABW's rare. Ended up hitting the Scheme's "Evil Win" condition.

I knew I was going to struggle with Poison Thanos.  His master strikes came at bad times in both games and my decks were decimated.  In both games I was unable to recover from this and build mu decks back up to get enough attack to reach him.  Then his symbiotic armor added to the number of attacks needed to bring him down.  For this setup I needed a Wolverine or two, but he was nowhere to be found.  This setup would be fun if it had a different mastermind - Killmonger or Ultron Infinity.

Psi-lord was an absolute MVP easily! I found a bystander in a deck and but it to the bottom and then when the bystander was alone, I could focus 1 star for 1 VP and 2 attack repeatedly with Interdimensional Rescue almost every turn once it got set up. It did stall the HQ after that, but it didn’t matter. I managed to KO 5 Master Strikes across both games, never getting hit with one in the on I submitted. There were a few turns where rescuing bystanders got me more recruit that I burned to rescue more bystanders. First time hitting the MM I got 37 attack and took 3 tactics. This scheme is almost trivial with Psi-lord.
I got my butt kicked through scheme Twists the first game, but Venompool really helped secure the 2nd

Digestion and Indigestion was such a fun mechanic to experience and it scales very well. Happy with the inclusion of Venom here. Beast felt like the missing puzzle piece for the strength and tech side. Overall a great time was had.

column36: We had two Phalanx Villains in the Victory Pile that we were able to pull out through Symbiote Bonds, including Bob's rare. Very early in the game, Taskmaster bonded with  the Mastermind and we both thought that was the game over right there. Luckily, Beast rules.

Game 1 felt like it was about to get away from me when from the light game an Adam Warlock that blasted the Carnage Rare Villain with its ability and 4 shards. From there, Carnage ate my whole deck and allowed me to generate shards every 2nd turn to win the game. It still took long because we had to clean up the city so Hybrid didn't get stuck on an awful minion to soft-lock me in the game but eventually, we were able to terrorize Hybrid with some Symbiote love.

We love the set up. It was a lot of fun. It was very slow at the beginning, but definitely ramped up once we got our attack going. one person focused on two cards with silk and spider Gwen and the occasional Bob annd Cap and the Devil, the other focused on Ronan Carnage and cap and the Devil. This led to large hands on both of our parts and made the game pretty smooth sailing. Very fun set up. We enjoyed it.

column36: Interesting setup with Hybrid's symbiotic strength and pumped up "Phalanx" heroes. I was able to manage the city, so that when Hybrid Master Striked he didn't bond with a extra strong Phalanx hero. I chose Solo to synergize with Excessive Violence from Carnage and Bob. I chose Venompool to synergize with Ronan (particularly bystanders) and overlap with a bit of Excessive Violence. Captain and the Devil supplemented the Bob and Carnage deck whereas Ronan and Venompool tried to focus on green and black hero classes. Solo's rare (able to cut a villain or mastermind in half) was key for dealing with pumped up Hybrid mastermind and pumped up Phalanx heroes. So much awkward bookkeeping tracking the 1/2 attacks and recruits! Struggled to conclusively pick two heroes, so I'm curious what others chose. Solo in general was OK, and his rare was crucial. Venompool overall was good with Ronan and I learned how awkward and annoying his "can't draw any more cards this turn" card is. Hard to tell if they were "optimal" choices though. I heavily considered Captain Britain as I thought he'd be a good all-around hero, particularly with so many different hero groups in the setup and would help satisfy Spectrum. I thought about trying hero(es) which could also move villains around. I ultimately didn't and glad I didn't worry about it. The Khonshu henchmen may be easier to fight on certain locations, but it ended up not being relevant too much because they were typically they preferred villain to be symbiote bonded with Hybrid.

column36: Being Halloween time and getting to fight zombies and vampires was a blast. The initial swarm had us worried, but once we got the hordes under control, it felt great. A+ all around!

First game, rough going with lots of struggle to control the City.  Second game, Ultron Sentries were more than half of the Villains entering the City, which meant lots of KO and a fast game, but maybe not so much VP.

This game, trying to keep the blood bank free made it take us a while to ramp up but when we did Rocket Racoon and Soul binding came good for us. These were some cards we haven't played as much with, so we were a bit more unsteady about our hero choices etc. but think we went okay once we got into the game. Our second game we had 3 master strikes straight off for wounds plus wounds from villains. We couldn't get our foot in the door - or attack villains in the city and it just kept going downhill from there. That game was just laughed off by us and we were grateful for the first game.

First game was extremely rough. We lost in 20 turns to the twist, barely scrapped 10 VP between us both as we were never able to clear the city. Second game was a very different story. We got Rocket's artifact in both hands early and a good number of Star-lords which gave us a good amount of shards. We chose werewolf by night for some of its KO prowess and drawing power, but it almost always came when there was sunlight in the HQ, so it's job was mostly rotating the HQ. Fun overall, but the first game was extremely frustrating
Rough games. First game lost before we could get going and the second we were on the cusp of losing before we could really control the city until it all came together. Shards for for the win.
Fun game with good synergy with hero choices. Annoyed to have 1 villain escape. Second attempt was done in fewer turns but more escapes which happened early due to several twists in a row.

This was a fun one! Having to use 1 team wasn't horrible just tricky sometimes. 
The different Masterminds were fun to use!  We thought we chose well this time for our heros as we normally struggle choosing.

The first game I had a lot of schemes and locations early, which helped me keep the city in check. I didn't have a lot of luck when it came to recruiting though, which added to my turn count. I decided it was best to end the game while leaving quite a bit of VP in the city and in the villain deck. Second game all of the villains came out to play. I was absolutely overrun at the beginning, which led to one escape before I got things under control. I was able to get Dethlok's uncommon and rare, which became the MVP of the game. She-Hulk came up big with her Hurl Trucks. I also got her rare, which gave me 9 attack one turn.
Second attempt was 6 turns faster, but I had 1 escape and lower points per turn by about .1 so I went with the first one. Which knowing my guessing skills is probably a mistake.

Normally I don't like games that depends pure on how lucky you are with the villain deck. However, this game I had the most luck I ever had on any other game. Everything worked great and the heroes that come to the HQ was exactly what I needed to make my strategy works.

This was a very strong 4. I think the Mastermind and Scheme paired quite nicely and I loved the gameplay. It was a simple race in terms of setup. I felt I picked two heroes that worked really well with the other heroes and in combating the challenge (being able to control the HQ a bit and get some Empowering). The Ranged synergy I was able to get with Vision and Wonder Man was super fun and Black Knight's rare with a 6 Attack card in my victory pile early on gave me all the firepower I needed. BK's Amulet of Avalon card was also fun to play when there was a larger HQ with lots of Strength cards. 

My first attempt reinforced my hero decisions and so the goal in my second run was to try and be faster. Very curious to see where I will land in the rankings this week (I think I did well) and which heroes everyone else went with. 

Another really fun setup!

Fun synergies everywhere with this setup. Hero choices really stuck out to me. Left side was a full on Range deck that used Wonder Man to his full power. Right side was a full Strength deck. Neither was super powerful, but didn’t need to be as I was able to manipulate Ragnarok into no more than a +2 or +4 bonus most of the game, especially as the HQ got “scared away.” Second attempt was slightly slower, but much higher ppt, so I hope that pays off!

Fun game! Wonder Man HQ manipulation really carried both games. Kept the mastermind in check and let me empower Wonder Man's own cards. First game his blue cards did most of the damage and the second game his green cards did. Ms America and Vision both worked well with him, but I didn't play a lot of Jessica Jones or Luke Cage.

Ended up having 2 good games. I had a slightly better PPT in game 1, but I managed to win game 2 quite a bit faster.

it becomes a race against the clock real fast, wonderman seemed great with a 8hq count but since the hq went down pretty fast in the early phase he soon became less useful.
lucky i got the victory in the end cuz any next twist wouldve been game over.
wouldve loved to get a 2nd try in cuz i know it could be a better game but don't rly have the time now or in the next 2 days so this'll have to suffice.
Very well done with synergy. Wish I had got off the ground faster though...

This was a really tough set of matches. Like I said in the discord my first game BOTH of my bystanders were under the 11th strike completely negating any effect they would have. The second game went a bit better. I was able to hold my own once I realized how crucial KO was going to be in this. That's why I brought in Carnage out of all the heroes I had access to still he had the best KO ability. Though the Doombots still did most of that work. The best part of this game though...becoming Worthy on multiple occasions thanks to Carnage. I think that might need to be illegal but it was still fun.

So much Throne's favor, so much fun! I just found out that my colleague has a 3D printer, so I asked him to print me a small throne for this setup, it was such a great feeling to use a throne and not a random object :D

We had some fun with this setup, finding some synergies with the given heroes and the ones we brought along. My partner had fun bouncing the Throne multiple times in a turn while I was using Thor and ranged cards to make my Mjolnir throws hit pretty hard. Maximus was annoying but we eventually got him down. My deck proved pretty worthy in the end!

Couples therapy! Black Bolt helped to strip down the starter cards fast, which I used to stack up on recruit and buy Lady Sid’s rare in the first couple turns. I had 6 twists in the first 12 turns, which didn’t help my chances against Maximus, but left the city empty until I could handle it. Dropping Storm for Sif gave the artifacts I needed, so I could bank cards from turn to turn and hit hard later. Often throwing them all when my deck was almost empty so I could draw them again the next turn. At the end I had just one more turn before the 11th twist would have dropped, so just made it. Really high turn count, but everything else looks good!

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: I was able to keep masterminds attack low by keeping high attack folks out of the HQ. Karnak was a good learning hero. Shatter the week point was pretty clutch once I got 4 of one group in my victory pile. Letting one hand mop those dudes up made this card really strong. I really like Karnak!

After two misplays (First time, I forgot to add my Bystanders to the villain deck and second time I hit Maximus without accounting for Abomination and like 5 turns passed before I caught it.), my two games were marginally different. The second game scored 1 less point but was 3 turns faster. Had Worthy on one side and Thrones Favor on the other. Had hoped to win sooner, but on a turn I could have ended, I cleared a 4-Villain city... then it was 5 turns before I could get that final hit again lol

It was definitely challenging. None of the heroes were great for recruiting so once a rare hero card came up in HQ with over 4 attack value, it boosted Maximus' with his highest abomination rule. It made it very difficult to get enough heroes in my deck to hit the mastermind. But in the second game I recruited shield officers and changed my heroes to carnage and venom which allowed me to draw cards, ko cards, and get valuable recruit and attack points with digest and excessive violence. It wasn't a terrible game but it went on long and was challenging to hit the mastermind. I feel like luck really plays a role in this scenario. If you pull a rare hero in the hq early you're in a lot of trouble.

column36: I think I recruited way too many cards. 3 Rares were in the HQ for most of the game, boosting Maximus by 5, but I could never get enough recruit to get them out of there!

Very interesting setup! I decided almost right away on a throne's favor hand, and a worthy hammers hand. The Thors didn't do too well in my first game but Medusa and Black Panther did insane with so much Throne's Favor. I ended up with both rares on that side and was able to gather plenty of attack. It took me a really long time to win but I'm hoping no escapes and a high PPT will help push me through. Fun!

We scaled quickly and the lack of MS kept Malekith within reasonable range the whole game. The second game we faced a lot of Squirrel Girl in the HQ and didn't scale quite as quickly.

Managed to get the Mirage/BRB combo going early game while the other side went with a Sunspot focus. The scheme required some extra thinking to work around, settled on Sif for the Artifacts that I can use when I want.

column36: Only had time for one attempt.  Never used Rival Overlords before and had 4 in the city before I realized they weakened each other instead of buffing (oop!).  But early twists made play really hard, even though I tried to choose heroes that would work even if I played most of the hand randomly.  Just never came together for me.  The asylum is a hard scheme, which I guess is why it is in my haven't beat it yet stack of twists.  Hero choice for this one was hard, as with the random psychotic break play, I didn't think What If? key word would be helpful / controllable.  Thank goodness we had sidekicks in this setup.  Maybe I should have gone with Sif over Wolfsbane and might have if I made the second attempt.  Very disappointed in my play and results.  Was ok fun.

When I noticed that the Mastermind gave bystanders, I had a veery simple idea: use Karma to dig down in the hero deck until I could get Apocolyptic Black Widow's Rare and use its Liberate to make short work of him. She really likes playing beside Beta Ray Bill anyway. It worked in game 1! Game 2 I won but it took much longer.
Having different scheme twist challenges kept you on your toes! It was nice to have Danger Sense with this scheme.
Havne't played much with the Endgame keyword, it was a fun element to navigate around.

Overall a fun setup but seemed like a normal game.  Took a bit to get some strength to start hitting hard.  Somehow ended up with the same VP for both players, so that was fun that both hands seemed to be doing about the same.

Really enjoyed this setup. Both games went okay. In my first play through, I had a nice start, but got blocked mid game with all high cost cards in the HQ and not enough recruit. Combined that with lots of Master Strikes early led to a very slow build. The second game was a little smoother. I didn't get as powerful, but I was more consistent and did a good job winning on the twists. In hindsight, I should've chosen to lose a few twists for the betterment of my hand.
Fun setup, fairly easy wins although we got pretty unlucky in the 2nd game and had 5 jurors against us before we knew it.  Managed to get control after that.
